subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 3
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For numeric entry questions, write only the number.

questions.questionText: The array below shows rows of stars. Which multiplication fact does it show?
questions.explanation: There are 5 rows with 7 dots in each row. 5 × 7 = 35.

questions.questionText: What is 6 × 7? Write only the number.
What is 45 ÷ 5?
Mrs. Park has 28 markers. She puts them in groups of 7. How many groups does she make?
questions.explanation: 28 ÷ 7 = 4 groups.

questions.questionText: What is 8 × 6? Write only the number.
Which number do these base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 5 hundreds, 2 tens, and 7 ones make 500 + 20 + 7 = 527.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is split into 4 equal parts. What fraction names ONE part?
questions.explanation: When a whole is split into 4 equal parts, each part is 1/4 of the whole.

questions.questionText: What is 74 rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.explanation: 74 is between 70 and 80. The ones digit is 4, so round down to 70.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 5 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 5/6 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 3/8 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is halfway between 7 and 8, and the minute hand points to 6, which means 30 minutes. The time is 7:30.

questions.questionText: How many sides does this trapezoid have?
questions.explanation: A trapezoid is a quadrilateral, so it has 4 sides.

questions.questionText: What is 350 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: 350 is exactly halfway between 300 and 400. The rule is to round up, so 350 rounds to 400.

questions.questionText: What is the area of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = width × height = 6 × 3 = 18 square cm.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 7 + 5 + 7 + 5 = 24 cm.

questions.questionText: What is 458 + 267?
A farm had 700 eggs. The farmer sold 365 eggs. How many eggs are left?
There are 9 fish tanks. Each tank has 4 fish. How many fish are there in all? Write only the number.
Which multiplication fact can help you solve 63 ÷ 9?
questions.explanation: Since 9 × 7 = 63, we know 63 ÷ 9 = 7.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 4 equal parts. The point is at the 3rd mark, which is 3/4.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 2/3?
questions.explanation: 2/3 of a bar shades the same amount as 4/6 of an equal bar. So 2/3 = 4/6.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: When a whole is split into fewer parts, each part is bigger. Thirds are bigger than fourths, so 1/3 > 1/4.

questions.questionText: Which whole number does the fraction 6/6 equal?
questions.explanation: When all 6 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, the whole is filled. 6/6 = 1.

questions.questionText: A poster is 9 cm wide and 5 cm tall. What is its area, in square centimeters? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: Area = 9 × 5 = 45 square cm.

questions.questionText: Which name best describes a shape with 4 equal sides and 4 square corners?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides and 4 right (square) corners.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 6 equal parts. The point is at the 2nd mark, which is 2/6.

questions.questionText: A class starts art at 1:25 and works for 40 minutes. What time does art end?
questions.explanation: From 1:25 add 35 minutes to reach 2:00, then 5 more minutes makes 2:05. So 1:25 + 40 minutes = 2:05.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: Both fractions have 5 shaded parts, but sixths are larger pieces than eighths, so 5/6 covers more. 5/6 > 5/8.

questions.questionText: A baker made 7 trays of rolls. Each tray held 8 rolls. He gave away 20 rolls. How many rolls are left?
questions.explanation: First find the total: 7 × 8 = 56 rolls. Then subtract: 56 − 20 = 36 rolls left.

questions.questionText: Each movie ticket costs $8. A group buys 6 tickets and pays with a $50 bill. How many dollars of change do they get back? Write only the number.
A rectangle has a length of 10 cm and a width of 2 cm. Which statement is true about this rectangle?
options: Its area is 20 square cm and its perimeter is 20 cm
Its area is 12 square cm and its perimeter is 24 cm
Its area is 24 square cm and its perimeter is 20 cm
Its area is 20 square cm and its perimeter is 24 cm
questions.explanation: Area = 10 × 2 = 20 square cm. Perimeter = 10 + 2 + 10 + 2 = 24 cm.

questions.questionText: Maria ate 2/8 of a pizza and Jon ate 1/4 of the same pizza. Who ate more?
questions.explanation: 2/8 and 1/4 cover the same amount of the pizza because 2/8 = 1/4. They ate the same amount.

questions.questionText: The table shows cans collected each day. How many more cans were collected on Thursday than on Monday? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: Thursday had 308 cans and Monday had 145. 308 − 145 = 163 more cans.

questions.questionText: A store has 6 shelves with 7 cans on each shelf. Then 5 more cans are added. Which expression shows the total number of cans?
questions.explanation: Multiply shelves by cans per shelf, then add the extra cans: 6 × 7 + 5 = 42 + 5 = 47.

questions.questionText: Select all expressions that equal 36.
Select all fractions that are equal to 1/3.
questions.explanation: 2/6 = 1/3 and 3/9 = 1/3. But 2/3 and 1/6 are not equal to 1/3.

questions.questionText: Select all numbers that are odd.
questions.explanation: A number is odd if its ones digit is 1, 3, 5, 7, or 9. 27 and 53 are odd. 40 and 64 are even.

questions.questionText: Select all numbers that round to 600 when rounded to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: 552, 640, and 610 are all closer to 600 than to 500 or 700. But 549 is closer to 500, so it rounds to 500.

questions.questionText: Select all rectangles that have an area of 18 square units.
What is 8 × 7? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: 8 rows of 7 dots is 8 × 7 = 56.
